scenarios.name: GPU thermal throttle + ECC double-bit error
scenarios.description: GPU 2 overheating causes thermal throttle, triggers uncorrectable ECC error, crashes vLLM pod with OOMKill during graceful shutdown. This is the primary demo scenario.
scenarios.expected_root_cause: GPU 2 entered thermal throttle at 91°C due to sustained high load and insufficient cooling. Thermal stress triggered an uncorrectable DRAM double-bit ECC error, which caused the CUDA driver to signal cudaErrorECCUncorrectable. The vLLM process attempted graceful shutdown but exceeded its 256Gi memory limit during drain, resulting in OOMKill. GPU 2 has been drained from the scheduler.
scenarios.business_impact: vLLM inference unavailable for ~3 minutes. 44 in-flight requests dropped. Throughput reduced by 48% for 4 minutes prior to crash.

scenarios.name: GPU out-of-memory on large batch
scenarios.description: A large batch request exceeds GPU VRAM, causing CUDA OOM. Pod crashes and restarts. No hardware fault — config issue.
scenarios.expected_root_cause: Triton inference server received a batch request with sequence length 8192 exceeding the configured max_batch_size memory budget. CUDA OOM on GPU 0. This is a configuration issue, not hardware failure.
scenarios.business_impact: Triton pod restarted once. 12 requests failed with 503. Model reload took 90 seconds.

scenarios.name: NVLink degraded — tensor parallel slowdown
scenarios.description: Persistent NVLink errors on GPU pair 2-3 reduce tensor parallel bandwidth. No crash but latency doubles.
scenarios.expected_root_cause: Accumulated NVLink replay errors on link 3 (GPU 2 <-> GPU 3) indicate potential physical connector issue or driver state corruption. Bandwidth reduced by ~35%. No ECC errors yet but trending toward hardware failure.
scenarios.business_impact: P99 inference latency increased from 450ms to 890ms. SLA breach if not resolved within 30 minutes.
